output:
Aggregated execute batches: completed 5/5.
Batch 1: Batch 1 executed for T1 and T2. Track reorder disabled-state logic now uses same-kind position, same-kind swap lookup now scans across opposite-kind boundaries, targeted lint passed on touched files, and the batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/fix-track-reordering-bugs-and-20260326-1856/execution_batch_1.json`.
Batch 2: Batch 2 executed. Same-kind track reorder logic is now covered by targeted vitest tests, and TimelineEditor now has nested track-level dnd-kit scaffolding with separate visual/audio SortableContext groups plus Pointer and Keyboard sensors. Targeted vitest and eslint passed, and the batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/fix-track-reordering-bugs-and-20260326-1856/execution_batch_2.json`.
Batch 3: Batch 3 executed. TrackLabel is now a sortable row with a hover-only `GripVertical` drag handle, and the old arrow-button reorder path has been removed from the video-editor hook/state/context flow in favor of `handleMoveTrack`. Targeted eslint passed, the existing reorder vitest suite still passed, and the batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/fix-track-reordering-bugs-and-20260326-1856/execution_batch_3.json`.
Batch 4: Batch 4 executed. The track-management test suite now covers the drag-based reorder helper, rejects cross-kind drag targets, and verifies that a serialize/buildTimelineData round-trip preserves within-kind ordering under the visual-before-audio invariant. The focused vitest file passed with 6/6 tests, targeted eslint passed, and the batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/fix-track-reordering-bugs-and-20260326-1856/execution_batch_4.json`.
Batch 5: Batch 5 executed as a validation-only pass. No additional code changes were needed for track reordering. Code inspection confirms the drag handle remains in the hover overlay, `isDragging` opacity is already applied, track-list scroll sync is still wired through `trackListRef` -> `timelineRef.setScrollTop`, and the nested track DnD remains scoped away from both the app-level DndContext handlers and the external file-drop handlers on `timelineWrapperRef`. `npx vitest run` was executed, but the repo-wide suite is currently red because of numerous pre-existing unrelated failures outside the track-reorder area. The batch checkpoint was written to `.megaplan/plans/fix-track-reordering-bugs-and-20260326-1856/execution_batch_5.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified by code inspection and targeted lint: `TrackLabel` now disables the up/down controls using `kindIndex` and `sameKindCount`, and `TimelineEditor` computes `kindIndex` from preceding same-kind tracks. This fixes the mixed-kind boundary case where the first audio track was incorrectly treated as movable upward based on global index.

Verified by code inspection and targeted lint: `handleReorderTrack` now scans in the requested direction until it finds the next track of the same kind before swapping. Mixed-kind boundaries now no-op when no same-kind target exists, while same-kind neighbors still reorder normally.

Extracted the arrow-button reorder logic into the pure helper `reorderTracksByDirection` and added a focused vitest suite that covers mixed kinds, single-of-each-kind, and single-kind-only configurations. Verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` passed with 3/3 tests.

TimelineEditor now wraps the track label list in a nested dnd-kit `DndContext`, uses distinct `PointerSensor` and `KeyboardSensor` instances with `sortableKeyboardCoordinates`, and renders separate visual/audio `SortableContext` groups. Drag-end handling validates same-kind moves before calling `handleMoveTrack`. Verification: targeted eslint passed on the touched TimelineEditor and hook/state files.

TrackLabel now uses `useSortable` with the sortable id passed from TimelineEditor, applies transform/transition styles plus `isDragging` opacity, and shows a single `GripVertical` handle in the existing hover overlay. The old ArrowUp/ArrowDown controls, `onReorder`, and the stale same-kind props/imports were removed. Verification: targeted eslint passed on the updated TrackLabel/TimelineEditor flow.

`handleMoveTrack(activeId, overId)` is the only exported track-reorder callback in the video-editor hook surface now. It reorders through `moveTrackWithinKind`, which validates same-kind moves before calling `arrayMove`, and all `handleReorderTrack` references were removed from the hook return value, state/context types, and TimelineEditor usage. Verification: targeted eslint passed and the existing reorder vitest file still passed.

Extended the existing track-management vitest file to cover the arrayMove-based drag reorder helper `moveTrackWithinKind`, including a successful same-kind move, a rejected cross-kind drag, and a serialize/buildTimelineData round-trip that confirms within-kind order survives load-time sorting. Verification: `./node_modules/.bin/vitest run --config config/testing/vitest.config.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts` passed with 6/6 tests, and targeted eslint passed on the hook/test files.
commands_run: rg -n "moveTrackWithinKind|serializeForDisk|buildTimelineData|preserves within-kind order|rejects drag moves|moves a track to a new position" src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineTrackManagement.test.ts

task_updates.executor_notes: No additional code changes were required. Verification by code inspection confirms: `TrackLabel` already applies `isDragging` opacity and keeps the grip handle in the hover overlay; the track list still syncs scroll position via `trackListRef`'s `onScroll` handler; the nested track `DndContext` remains scoped to the track-list column while the app-level DnD handlers stay on the outer app shell; and the external file-drop handlers remain attached to `timelineWrapperRef`, outside the nested track DnD subtree. `npx vitest run` was executed but does not pass repo-wide because of numerous unrelated existing failures outside the video-editor track-reorder area.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. `TrackLabel` now checks `kindIndex === 0`, and `TimelineEditor` computes `kindIndex` by counting preceding same-kind tracks. In `[V1,V2,A1,A2]`, `A1` has global index 2 but `kindIndex` 0, so the up button disables correctly.
Yes. The reorder handler now scans for the next same-kind track before swapping. In `[V1,V2,A1,A2]`, moving `V2` down finds no later visual track and no-ops; moving `A1` down finds `A2` and swaps.

Yes. The new test file covers all three required configurations: mixed kinds `[V1,V2,A1,A2]`, single-of-each-kind `[V1,A1]`, and single-kind-only `[V1,V2,V3]`. The reorder logic is now directly testable as the pure helper `reorderTracksByDirection`.

Yes. TimelineEditor now contains two separate `SortableContext` groups, one for visual tracks and one for audio tracks, inside a nested `DndContext` configured with both `PointerSensor` and `KeyboardSensor` using `sortableKeyboardCoordinates`.

Yes. `TrackLabel` now renders a `GripVertical` handle inside the existing hover overlay, and the old ArrowUp/ArrowDown buttons plus their related props/imports are removed. Targeted eslint passed on the updated component wiring.

Yes. `handleMoveTrack` reorders through `moveTrackWithinKind`, which checks that both tracks share the same kind before calling `arrayMove`, and `handleReorderTrack` has been fully removed from the video-editor hook/context surface.

Yes. The new round-trip test reorders tracks with `moveTrackWithinKind`, serializes the reordered config with `serializeForDisk`, rebuilds it with `buildTimelineData`, and verifies the rebuilt track order remains `['V2', 'V1', 'A1', 'A2']`, confirming that `sortTracksByKind` preserves the user's within-kind ordering.

By code inspection, yes: the track list still calls `timelineRef.current?.setScrollTop(...)` from `trackListRef`'s `onScroll`, and track dragging is contained within the nested `DndContext` mounted inside the track-list column while the app-level DnD handlers remain on the outer app shell. The external file-drop handlers also remain attached to `timelineWrapperRef`, outside the nested track DnD subtree. I could not perform an interactive browser drag session in this environment, so this validation is static plus test-run based rather than a live UI interaction.
